title: Memory recall
description: Read-only recall over the engine's memory ledger — the project's narrative "how did I get here?" record (past decisions, the pushback and rationale behind them, lessons, dead-ends, preferences, intents). Every conforming implementation answers the same recall operation, so a session can ask "what do we already know about this?" and get the relevant past episodes ranked best-first, without knowing whether the offline keyword floor or a richer meaning-based engine is answering. It returns narrative recall only and never structural fact (knowledge's job); a caller that wants the entities a memory references composes that itself by filtering on an entity-id tag at read time.

operations.name: search
operations.description: Recall the memory records most relevant to a query, ranked best-first. An optional role filter narrows to record kinds (the closed memory role vocabulary: decision, rationale/pushback, lesson, dead-end, preference, intent, observation); an optional tag filter narrows to records carrying given tags (entity references such as eADR-#### or free topic tags — the read-time link to knowledge is composed by filtering on an entity-id tag); an optional limit caps how many results are returned.

query.description: The recall query text, matched against the records' narrative content.

roles.description: Restrict to these record roles (the closed memory role vocabulary: decision, rationale/pushback, lesson, dead-end, preference, intent, observation). Omit for all roles. Validated against the implementation's own role schema, not frozen by this contract.

tags.description: Restrict to records carrying any of these tags (entity references such as 'eADR-0007' or free topic tags). Omit for no tag filter.

limit.description: The maximum number of ranked results to return. Omit for the implementation's default.

results.description: The matching memory records, ordered best-first by relevance (the ranking is the implementation's — BM25 for the lexical floor, reinforced by usage). Each result is a record object whose exact shape is the memory substrate's own (role, narrative, tags, provenance, score), not frozen by this contract.

recall_completeness.description: An optional plain-language note, present only when results are returned, telling the reader the results are curated summaries whose original word-for-word notes are still kept and recoverable — so the reader can offer the operator the exact wording. Omitted when there are no results. Whether the note is present is not frozen by this contract; the completeness disclosure floor itself is enforced elsewhere, not by this key.
